channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 147 replies — 588.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 147 • Updated: 2026-07-11 04:14 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62460654
>>62449961
Most strip malls have a staffing agency that would jump if anyone without a felony came in. It'll suck at the beginning but that's what you get for wasting 23 years of your life.

62460716
>>62460633
?

uber drivers crack $40/hour where I am

62460737
>>62460716
Well, that's unusual. They're getting very lucky.

62460759
>>62460737
I’d be surprised if they don’t make at least $25 an hour in most metro areas and the peripheries

where do you live if you don’t mind my asking that they are legitimately only making minimum wage?

62460789
>>62460759
>make $25 an hour
>spend double that on gas and vehicle maintenance.
How exactly is that supposed to pan out?

62460802
>>62460789
maintenance isn’t that much if you have a newer car, and I’m pretty sure driving some shit box is against uber rules anyway you need to register with a relatively new car


gas eh maybe you have a point but they’re still out so it’s obviously profitable

I highly doubt uber drivers are making minimum wage except in some super rural area where everyone had a car. And I know for a fact they make decent money where I live

62461444
>>62460802
So I need a high paying job to get a nice new car so I can ruin it working for a lower paying job?
I live in California, gas alone would fuck me sideways. If I had a nice car I'd leave. Go to Oregon or Washington.

62461781
I just realized I'm going to need to learn how to pay taxes.

62462300
>>62461781
https://www.irs.gov/individuals/free-tax-return-preparation-for-qualifying-taxpayers if you make less than 69k a year you probably qualify

62462365
trucking. self-driving trucks are a meme and still decades away, if you're a company driver you can pull down 80k clean with little overhead.

most companies will hire you on the spot if you have a CDL and a clean record

62462409
>>62459478
yeah you arre going to have to drive miles to your job
you DO have a car right op?

62462413
>>62462365
>if you have a CDL
they have pivoted to only hiring jeets as of a year or two ago

62462416
>>62460802
it's all jeets and other browns that cheat the system by having 3 drivers share the same car and uber account
you're lucky to make minimum wage after gas, tax and wear and tear

62462448
>>62462416
Actually, sharing a car makes sense. You at least split the cost of buying a car between you. And the insurance.

62463196
>>62462448
Triple the maintenance, but you have support from your other drivers

62464516
Is playing the lottery viable?

62465774
Sorry, I need the thread to stay up a little longer.

62465843
>>62449961
Similar to you OP, 40 years old, only 1 year office job experience.
Although I did learn programming in my free time. I am also taking care of me and my mother's estate which includes 3 rental properties and a sizable stock portfolio. I also taught myself ancient Greek, some Japanese, and some Russian. I read about business and finance daily.
I'm pretty sure I could be useful in an office setting, especially one finance or real estate related. Not necessarily because of my real estate or finance knowledge, but by being very comfortable using unix utilities, setting up servers, writing programs and scripts, and setting up web services. That, in addition to understanding, tax, finance, and renting.

I just don't know how to frame all these for an employer. I'm thinking it'd be easier to start an online agency.

62466261
>>62465843
You sound like you've got it made compared to me. Good luck on your agency.

62466338
>>62449961
Close your eyes.
Imagine a career.
Now make it into a resume.
There is no employment gap.

62468866
And, bump

62468886
Work should be vocational. Just do voluntary stuff that you feel is important or do your own thing. Anything else makes one miserable.

62468898
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2f4qnU1oOoc

62468936
>>62449961
I'm in a similarish position to this guy. I'm 38 and I'm thinking of becoming an actuary. I'm a math PhD dropout (have a master's I guess but I never bothered to apply for graduation) and I've basically been NEETing since. Work part time in a warehouse but that's not relevant to anything. Now someone my age switching to actuary isn't unheard of but usually they have a continuous record of experience in something that can give them an advantage, like being a teacher or w/e. I haven't taught since grad school which was a decade ago. I have nothing to show for my gap. Unironically how fucked am I? I have no chance of being hired recent grads because of my gap that only possibly be interpreted as laziness and apathy right? Passing the first round of exams would be easy but it costs money and it's likely for nothing if I don't get hired.

62469039
>>62449961
Felling like sharing my story with you OP:

I've dropped out of college in 2016 because I learned too little too late that the degree I was aiming was worthless.

After some shitty jobs, I moved in another country to be a traditional gunsmith (old lath, files,eating steeldust daily, basically turning raw material to toys for adult) but I wasn't very good at boot licking my teachers.I did acquire all the skills I need but the last thing they do to fuck me in the ass was to denied me the diploma for shady reasons.

Went back to my hometown, did a lot of different jobs (from manufacturing to car insurance assistant) while trying to figure out a way to be a gunsmith and I did found one.

Moved out from my dad's home (so he could not drink 1/4 of my paycheck anymore) for a city 200km away, I was happy since I could finally trying to live a normie life testing guns, even for a pay way below what I was providing to the company (+140€ every two month for risking getting shrapnel'd alone in a soundless booth) and it lasted 8 months before they found the level of lead in my bloodstream was too high (apparently air evacuation was failing and they didn't feel the need to inform me) and I was fired for "incapacity to do the job".

Moved back with my mother to get healthy again and I did, but apparently my resume scare the normies, even when I try to lie or sweeten it, so since I live in a touristic area with old farts waiting for the sweet embrace of the reaper and turbowelfares negros of all colors, I tried to get into another trade but the joboffice providing refused 9 of my business plans so I went basic and started by buying a van to sell drinks.

The van was a scam and I'm at the edge of being a welfare-wigger.

I'm 30, I hate my communist shithole of a country (France), I had more than 40 different jobs and I don't even know what to do anymore beside learning trading and workout.

Just don't give up.

62469070
>>62468866
With respect kill yourself.
Your excuse for not applying to jobs is "it's 4am fuckface" but you keep waking up at fucking 6pm and going to sleep at 8am.
You're not helping yourself at all stop pretending and stop bumping this fake engagement thread (blog).

62469080
just say your a nigger or poopjeet

62469085
I am 39 and haven’t had a job in over 10 years..

62469146
nigger just do what foids do get a comfy wfh hr, middle manager, project manager, customer liason, customer service or other phone operator job.

62469410
We shouldn't have to go to some corporation just to get an income.

item.title: >shitty particle board bedroom dresser is disintegrating >look into buying something nice, made of hardwood >$3-5k avera… — 63 replies — 180.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
>shitty particle board bedroom dresser is disintegrating >look into buying something nice, made of hardwood >$3-5k avera…

Open thread • Replies: 63 • Updated: 2026-07-11 03:59 UTC

OP

>shitty particle board bedroom dresser is disintegrating
>look into buying something nice, made of hardwood
>$3-5k average price for a single bedroom dresser (not a set)
is this economic progress? a SINGLE ITEM of hardwood bedroom furniture, something that was commonplace 50 years ago, is now a luxury good, to the extent that it costs like 10% of the annual post-tax wages of the average american worker?

Early replies (first 3)

62466672
Fiat debases the value of work even compared to wood

62466682
>>62466672
it's truly insane. i'm not looking for like something made of continuous slabs of old growth redwood. $3k is basically the price floor on something made of commodity grade oak or whatever.

62466688
>>62466682
You are paying for the labor costs.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62467282
>>62466665
Should've kept you grandparents furniture

62467356
>>62466665
You will buy the Ikea dresser made of 1/4 inch thick particle board, and you will be happy

62467372
>>62467122
Such is life every time you step outside the front door in the modern world.

62467381
>>62466783
If you think woodworking is so easy, why don't you do it yourself. Oh, wait... You can't? Then supply and demand it is.

62467483
>>62466665
it's crazy isn't it? I almost bankrupted myself buying a single amish side table. The quality is excellent but I can't justify a matching one.. To me, the price of wood shit is insane given all the land and forests here.

62467728
>>62466699
>>62466688
Correct. That and shipping. Hardwood costs have also increased. And then you're also dealing with premium hardwood manufacturing at something less than scale.
>>62466665
How many hardwood dressers do you think a middle earning family 200 years ago had? Do you think they bought it, or made it themselves or inherited it? For how many generations would it be in the family? Where's the lack of progress?

62467770
>>62467728
>How many hardwood dressers do you think a middle earning family 200 years ago had?
did particle board exist 200 years ago?

62467803
>>62467770
Cheap garbage practical furniture always existed anon, only the nice hardwood stuff gets preserved.

62467814
>>62466823
you sleep on the floor and just now realize you are poor??? lmao

62467904
>>62466665
How often are you looking to rebuy the dressers
Like once every 2-3 years?
Or rather going for that yearly refresh?

62467916
>>62467065
It doesn't work like in Ferngully where the heckin forests are being logged for wood.
99% of Western wood demand is served by planted tree farms.
(Note: this is only about the civilized world)

62467937
>>62466823
My most expensive piece of furniture right now is my futon that was made in the country I live.
It's a thin Japanese style one so I basically sleep in the floor too.

62467954
>>62467916
Yeah because the deforestation already happened in the west. Retard

62468034
>>62466665
I bought a house recently and wanted to get a dresser that would last me so I chose real hardwood. It's high quality and heavy, but the fucking faggot makers of the dresser give you cucked, short rails that only allow the drawers to open halfway. I guess one retard opened the drawers all the way and it tipped and now we all have to suffer? Either way, returning it would of been too much of a hassle so I replaced the drawer rails and now the drawers open fully. Of course none of the retards reviewing it mentioned how the drawers don't slide out all the way.

62468057
>>62466823
Welcome in the low status male club, chud.

62468164
>>62466823
For me it's a little different. It makes me realize that I'm downwardly mobile. My parents had nice furniture when we were kids and still have nice furniture. They were able to afford a house and a few kids after ~5 years of marriage.

I'm in my mid-30's, can't afford a house or nice furniture. I keep telling myself things will get better but my student loan is like a chain ball locked to my leg.

Not sure how I'm going to escape lower-middle class life, it'll actually take a miracle.

62468192
Should I pick up making custom cabinetry as a side hustle?

62468302
>>62468192
Its not a side hustle, despite being a box with a door the hard part is fitting them into not-exactly-square rooms. If you half ass it, it will look and perform like an indian did it

62469259
>>62466665
what you do instead is buy 4,000 dollars worth of shop equipment and make your own furniture like men in the past did.

62469287
>>62467803
>only the nice hardwood stuff gets preserved.
Hardwood is a botanical term, there's lots of wood which while it's 'hardwood' is softer and shittier than softwood.

62469290
>>62467954
Actually the US is more heavily forested than 150 years ago.

62469299
>>62468164
>Not sure how I'm going to escape lower-middle class life, it'll actually take a miracle.
Have you attempted grabbing your bootstraps and lifting?

62469301
>>62468302
can't that be done with small screw-adjustable standoffs and some filler material? does it really take more than that?

62469303
>>62466665
i have particle board stuff thats lasted a long time and looks nice. makes me wonder what that one boomer was ranting about all those years ago about particle board sucking when mine has lasted for a long time, a decade plus

62469309
>>62466665
hardwood is not for poors.
this is economic progress.
brown ids get out of this thread.

62469312
>>62469303
Particle board only lasts 20-30 years usually. Hardwood you only have to buy once or get it for free from dead relatives. Seriously The wood will grow back by the time someone needs to replace it. By the way there is a middle ground of interlocked boards instead of one wide piece.

62469321
>>62468164
im retardmaxxing, what other choice do i have? slavery into my 60s/70s? no fucking thanks. glad im not in europe where so many nations are unanimously installing suspiciously similar laws, ie pic related or moving forward with deporting ukrainian male refugees back to ukraine

62469326
>>62469299
Now that's good advice

62469342
>>62466665
Facebook marketplace

62469383
>>62466665
It used to be a lot more than that comparatively
But if you want some quality hardwood dressers look on craigslist or other apps people use to get rid of stuff.
There are plenty of people trying to get rid of stuff but its going to be real heavy

item.title: Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late. — 98 replies — 109.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Open thread • Replies: 98 • Updated: 2026-07-11 03:27 UTC

OP

Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Early replies (first 3)

62466535
They should just make corporate espionage legal in western countries at this point. The only people who are winning from the current laws are the Chinese

62466548
>>62466527
>state media says
lol

62466549
>>62466546
Show me one innovation from Japan.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62467824
Oh nice, took them long enough to steal the right data.

62467835
>>62467366
Shut the fuck up you chink worshipping faggot

62467844
>>62467824
ai is an arms race to steal as much data as possible from people the world over
chinese ai specifically is being catered to stealing all THAT data to parse it for advancement of the state apparatus (because commies). Its getting better at finding the better data now, but soon enough they'll just exhaust all the shit they can steal and nobody will want to innovate anymore and we'll have an age of ai hallucinated 'discovery' with predictably underwhelming (and often highly disastrous) results
Welcome to the chinese century

62467881
>>62466527
esl thread alert
>until it's too late
before it's too late

62468089
>>62467819
They are absolutely in direct competition. The main end product is connectivity, and that product is sold on the global market. Government contracts are only a fraction of SpaceX's revenue.

62468142
>>62468089
I didn't say government contracts. I'm talking about the deep link between government policy and corporate rollout. AI leadership is a national security concern and so companies like Nvidia and anthropic are deep in bed with the US government on what they can and can't do. See chip export restrictions and mythos lockdowns. So when the question becomes "which company do we trust to launch our highly proprietary hardware and models into space?" The answer is never ever going to be a Chinese company when forced IP disclosure is baked into the deal of working with Chinese state industries.
Same goes for China. They're making these rockets because they'll never in a million years leave their access to space gatekept by American foreign policy. The competition is on a government level and on foreign countries that want access to space. But that market is laughable. Only America and China have any real business in space and neither will buy from the other.

62468165
>>62466535
Thank you Elon very cool

62468190
>>62468142
Of course US companies will be banned from launching on Chinese rockets; this has already been the case since the 1990s.

The point is that Starlink will be in competition with China's Qianfan and Guowang constellations in third countries, which is the bulk of the connectivity market by value. China's reusable rockets are an input good into Qianfan and Guowang, since they will be used to launch en masse.

>forced IP disclosure is baked into the deal of working with Chinese state industries
No it isn't. It depends on the context and on the negotiating power of the Chinese company.

62468230
>>62468190
I disagree but time will tell.

62468297
>>62466527
>>62467881
Was going to point this out.

>Sell until it's too late
>"until it's too late"

Such a pathetically lazy ESL shill/FUD thread, but at least there's a learning opportunity here. Some things to note:

1) It's suspicious (and perhaps pathetic) how many people read the OP and responded in earnest without calling out the obvious ESL-slop OP.

2) It took what, like ~70 replies before someone called out the OP and pointed out the blatant ESL syntax?

3) There are several possibilities why this has happened, but three of the most probable are A) this board has been overrun with ESL retards who opine on American companies and American economics as if they know what they're talking about; B) blatant shill/FUD threads are started by foreigners and responded to by bots or engagement farms in order to boost signal in attempts to affect American investments/companies/assets/etc.; or C) a combination of A) and B).

4) This is why flags are so important. This board is gimped due to a lack of knowledge. We should be able to know whether the person giving us advice on American stocks is posting from Pakistan or Bulgaria or Ukraine. It's actually negligent to have a "business/finance" board without flags.

62468335
>>62468297
For example, when I post on a board with flags (/sp/), it becomes very clear very quickly that foreigners posting on this website hate the United States at an irrational level. They have almost no interactions with Americans in real life and their only exposure to the United States is through media, mostly online.

So when they opine on the United States, our culture, our sports, our education, our technology/business/economics, or even just our daily lives, they often do it from a place of pure resentment, spite, and almost no actual knowledge of the United States whatsoever. Everything they spew about the United States is either propaganda from whatever retarded state-run media they're forced to adhere to, or vile hearsay from some social media figure they've latched onto (also propaganda btw).

Although /sp/'s average IQ level is (maybe?) below this board's average, I have no doubt the foreigners here share the same resentment towards the United States and do not post about American companies/business from a neutral perspective.

I like hearing foreign perspectives by the way, but I deserve to know when I'm speaking with a foreigner, in order to put the things I'm reading into perspective. For example, OP's ESL-syntax plus the content of the post was a dead giveaway that he's posting this out of pure resentment.

62468346
Glad I didn’t buy space x stock

62468390
>>62468335
Fair points, but whenever possible, I try to deal with people in good faith (its a white trait, or flaw, depending on the situation)
The only way out of a low trust civilization is to deal with others in good faith, and violently expelling them permanently if they step out of line. Sadly, we can't do the latter half of that anymore and so here we are

62468428
>>62468297
>>62468335
What? Sorry I couldn't hear you I think you're breaking out? KRRSSHRHSHH KRRSHH...hat? Sorry could yoKRRRSCHH KRRSCH...peat that?

62468437
Holding a bag of spcx $210

62468446
>>62468297
Totally agree. I just assume all other posters are Indian as a default at this point. Mods complain about spam but won't do the one thing that would kill spam forever. Flags and range bans.

62468568
>>62468297
Nigga, your national debt is owned by Asian and Middle Eastern shitholes. Shut your bitch mulatto mouth.

62468589
>>62468568
>Nigga
>your national debt

ESL talking like a nigger. Another reason we need flags.

62468596
>>62468335
I lived in the USA for most of a decade and I hate you just as much

62468632
>>62468596
I don't remember asking, but thanks for your input. I'll add it to the list of reasons we need flags.

62468768
>>62466527
He boghted the spacesex ponzi ? kek

62468783
>>62466527
SPCX sisters?

62468833
>>62468568
25% is foreign owned in the form of bonds. Read a book

62468851
>>62466548
you know all the big news agencies of your country are state media right? It's just more covert

62468864
>>62466905
>They're only good at copying
Americans will say this while replying on a Chinese manufactured phone, then have an oolong tea, hit reply, put the phone down, and then eat a Chinese Boy Piss Egg with chopsticks and say we don't make anything new.

62468867
>>62468851
Such as? Name them, cunt.

62468872
>>62468864
Are you ok?

62468987
>>62466549
anime girls

62469293
>>62466527
cuz elon hires chinks and poos who will bring back or sell the tech to china

62469347
>>62466610
It's a very interesting story, but a part that is easy to forget is that he had an investor fully bankrolling his efforts for multiple years with $3 millions.

item.title: BEARISH ON COSTCO — 141 replies — 77.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
BEARISH ON COSTCO

Open thread • Replies: 141 • Updated: 2026-07-11 02:31 UTC

OP

People are scamming costcos lenient return policy now. Should we short COST?

Video:
https://x.com/Basedgoymemes/status/2073860759269077199

Early replies (first 3)

62449396
based

62449400
I wouldn't call jeets 'people'

62449441
Fuck this racist, post the person who started to do this was some white bitch, she did it for 12 years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62460624
>>62449441
Bloody bitch bastard

62460700
>>62449388
this is old news
I do this.
>18 months later mattress topper starts to hurt my back
>Whats the reason for the return?
>it hurts my back
>ok thank you sir, here is full refund

or the guy who returned his inflatable pool after a few months. He got the pool for his pet penguin that he ordered. after a few months and no penguin delivery he realized it was a scam and returned the pool and other things he got for the penguin.
>reason for return
>penguin order was a scam, no penguin arrived

I also know some dudes who buy wavestorms, go fuk em up, and exchange them

Costco puts the onus of returns on the manufacturer or supplier as a price for getting on the shelf in the store. just having a spot in costco is worth it enough to most companies to front all the returns for costco's lax return policy. Which is why costco DGAF and rather have customers.

62460720
>>62458144
serious question
I went bowling last week. There was a huge group of brown men. Enjoying themselves, very much, maybe too much.
They can not keep their hands off each other it was the strangest thing I've every seen.
Rubbing, patting, petting....
Each time 1 went to bowl they had to break free of all these hands and arms.
It was like a race to be the last one to touch the bowler, right up until they've thrown. like the hands and arms in drag me to hell expect brown and sensual
so bizarre.
why do they do this?
pic rel but brown and stinky

62460768
>>62455914
I for one love my country. And like true love I hate all those that threaten to jeopardize it. Enjoy your stay but you have to go back

62460799
fun fact: indians skin is brown because they each eat up to 4lbs of poop daily, like how flamingos turn pink from their diet of shrimp!

62460937
>>62460799
Oh I thought flamingos turned pink because they ate pussy

62461696
saaaaaaaaaaaars

62462254
>>62460799
wtf

62462262
>>62449388
All these office chairs are basically painted fabric. The paint flakes off after a while. They shouldn't be able to sell this junk in the first place.

62462271
>>62449388
>People
americans are not people.

62462287
>>62449441
Why do Indians talk so weird? It's like you can immediately tell they're jeets just from text alone

62462621
>>62462271
Seethe

62462950
>>62449388
I've seen people abuse their return program, and get their membership canceled.

62462960
>>62453201
i would cover her face with semen if you catch my drift

62463582
>>62449441
BENCHOOOOOD!!!!!!! WHY YOU REDEEM!! NO NO DO NOT CLICK ON THAT!!!! NOOOOOOOOOOO!!!!!!

62463635
>>62458606
cucks here won't do shit, they bitch and complain while patronizing Indian establishments. They will talk about the take over and then also about their favorite totally awesome pajeet they met at signhortons. The most opposition you will get is from white canadians. People will whine about the state of tim hortons while simultaneously pretending a coffee from there is a mandatory purchase they will die without

Sad, but they will have to watch canada just disintegrate with no option to fix it before they maybe realize. This whole country is full of just chicken shit low IQ cattle. Maybe 4% of canadians realize how bad this actually is and are doing the bare minimum like not hiring and giving money to the invaders, the rest are fully, entirely complicit in it. If it wasn't for how big this place was, that you can find thousands of acres with not a single person within 5km of you, I would have already left.

62464135
>>62463582
Saaars

62464324
>>62463635
Canada is just India now accept it

62464711
>>62462960
Kek

62464984
>>62449441
kek'd at the replies

62464997
>>62449441
I fucking hate Indians so much man

62465006
>>62449846
lol. Source?

62465010
>>62449441
easy (you)s

62465617
>>62465010
Cope

62465950
>>62449388
+1000 izzat

62466351
saaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaar

62467929
ssaaaaaaaaars!

62468640
>>62465950

62468814
>>62449388
went yesterday, some pajeeta was loading a plastic bag with ice from the soda machine

62469260
waiting for the dump

item.title: Is it true that we can cure the affordability crisis and inflation if we all just buy stonks? — 43 replies — 69.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is it true that we can cure the affordability crisis and inflation if we all just buy stonks?

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-07-11 03:43 UTC

OP

Is it true that we can cure the affordability crisis and inflation if we all just buy stonks?

>OK, final thought. Um, record high in the stock market. You may know that. Oil prices coming down. That was easily predictable. And they’ll continue to drop. We hope they go below $70 a barrel.

>Now, the American oil companies will take their sweet time because they want to make as much money as possible, from us, dropping at the pump, but it’ll happen. But the most important thing is that, about 40% of Americans have no stocks, no mutual funds, no IRA, nothing.

>Okay. 58% according to Gallup have stocks. This is how you grow your money in a capitalist society.
Now if you don’t have any stocks, what deduce do you think is going to happen to you when you get older? What do you think that the government is going to provide a comfortable living for you? Is that what you think?

>You’ll get Social Security, but that eeks by. You want to live in a trailer?

>Stock market is the way to amass capital. And I’m not a speculator. I just tell my guys, I have a financial advisor and you know, you have to have one by the S&P, the Standard & Poors. That means that by and just hold it. My portfolio is doing great because Trump, his policies, appeal to the big companies, but 40% of Americans don’t participate in capitalism and a lot of them are yowling about high prices?
Come on. You get 4% inflation here. Live in the real world. So you younger people, you got to get into the stock market. Don’t take big risks. Go with the conservative stuff. Final thought.

Early replies (first 3)

62459793
Its an income problem. People arent even buying fastfood as much anymore.

62459803
>>62459782
The guy you're quoting is a nepobaby coke addict.

62459807
Is it?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62460061
>>62460013
I boated oil today usa out of vc 2 days ago was the sign of the jew, man jews are ez nowadays.

62460067
>>62460013
Be thankful the markets already shit or youd have really gotten fucked

62460107
>>62459926
>>62459931
>Everyone and everything just needs less regulation and more privatization
How, in your mind, does that solve anything? How would privatizing social security make it better?

>you need mass production
Of what? What can we make that isn't already meeting the demand for it?
"Just make shit"? What? Tables? Hula hoops?

62460134
>>62460107
Put what I posted into Chat-GPT and tell it to steelman my argument. Ask it more questions if you're interested. AI does a fairly good job steelmanning arguments if you specifically tell it to.

>you need mass production
>Of what? What can we make that isn't already meeting the demand for it?
Housing, healthcare, energy, vehicles, literally everything people online complain about on a daily basis.

62460557
>the way you earn money isn't by providing a useful service or labor its by purchasing pieces of paper that arbitrarily rise in value because a board lied to retards about what they actually do
How do people think this shit is actually stable or even good? How retarded do you have to be to reach the conclusion that having more money to start and letting it sit somewhere and doing nothing yourself is actually the healthiest output for society? If your primary way to raise your status in society is to just throw money at the pre-existing largest companies then you've just damned all future generations into haves and have-nots off the misfortune of their birth which inherently destabilizes shit if the underclass ever gets uppity

62460637
>>62459782
>Now if you don’t have any stocks, what deduce do you think is going to happen to you when you get older? What do you think that the government is going to provide a comfortable living for you? Is that what you think?
bill is going to be 90, I'm going to lead my fellow /biz/bros and eat him cannibal style lol.

>>62459793
just buy stocks and then use the stocks as collateral to buy fast food r-tard.

62460640
>>62460013

62460804
>>62460637
You aren't leading shit and if you came near my land me and my senpai would shoot you.

62460813
>>62460804
No one cares boomer, you're an old man you can't even withstand the blowback from your own gun and there is a whole legion of us that want to eat your flesh.

62460820
>>62460813
*yawn* I'm probably your age and I'm not getting recruited into your fag mob. Nobody is.

62460887
>>62459782
Yeah, it's true. The reason this mess exists in the first place is because of the 2008 financial crisis mind fucking an entire generation. They got spooked and bought all the housing, traumatized by the stock market. Housing mooned and now everything is a mess. The funny thing is, if you bought stocks in 2009, the gains would have been much more than you'd ever get buying real estate at the time. Just as it historically always does.

62462852
>>62460804
>>62460820
The Boomer hatred and generational warfare shit is retarded.

Nobody will ever give a fuck about long-term fixes to problems. They want short-term feel-good garbage, which is why reddit commies and wignat incels are taking over the internet discourse. None of them will actually contend with what --> >>62459926 >>62459931 said. It's easier to just say "murder everyone I don't like" instead of actually getting into the economic details.

62462859
Stop being poor it's the golden age of merica

62464699
>>62462852
Philip of Macedon, Caesar Augustus, Julius Caesar, Pericles, Solon, Lycurgus, Cincinnatus, George Washington—all cared more about the long term than gratifying the appetites or egos, and got others to as well. Not to mention Aristotle (tutor or Alexander and Ptolemy), Plato (teacher of Aristotle) or Socrates (teacher of Plato and Xenophon) and Isocrates, or the many other men of wisdom.

Hell, even the great men who were a bit erratic like Alexander the Great showed they cared plenty about the future and carefully planned for the COMMON GOOD, they just didn't live long enough (Auerelian) or had some vices (Alexander, Napoleon).

62464715
>>62464699
Washington was actually called the American Cincinnatus early on, and he, Hamilton, and Jefferson balanced out to be our Solon. Unfortunately, Trump is our Nero or Elagabalus, or more realistically, like a retarded old Boomer version of Sula .

62465229
>>62459926
Deport the nonwhites

62465244
>>62462852
Killing your father to secure your future is natural and why tribes would leave old people in the mountains when they were burdnes.

You can get shot in the face to defend some old fucks when society starts breaking down but I'm gonna loot their mcmansions.

62466231
>>62459782
Ok so this fucker is spamming now
Bypassing the bureaucratic red tape for my kid. One of them qualifies for the Trump savings accounts, the other is completely locked out by arbitrary eligibility rules. Standard gov incompetence. Instead of letting one child start life behind, I'm taking control and building a dedicated brokerage account myself to put $1k straight into boring, long-term index funds. The cap is strictly $1,000—not asking for millions like the rest of the idiots on here. Toss a few bucks if you want to help fund a real future outside of government control.
https://gofund.me/e03ae854c

62466266
>>62460107
>Of what? What can we make that isn't already meeting the demand for it?
America has a notoriously aged and crumbling existing infrastructure combined with lagging development in new infrastructure. Both of those areas need to be addressed with New Deal types of public works projects that create jobs and improve the economy and lives of everyone.

62466272
>>62466231
A 529 plan is better anyways. Just give the kid who isn't eligible for a trump account an extra $1k in his 529

62466335
>>62459782
Goddamnit
Yet another top signal

62467010
>>62459926
>Just get rid of all pollution laws and lets billionaires do whatever they want bro. Just dissolve all obligations between employers and leaders and the rest of society. Just plow a solid share of common people's income into a stock market where the rich own over 90% of all stonks and can insider trade with impunity and rug pull them. That'll fix everything.

Remember class, the solution to the ills of liberalism and modernite ideology is always MORE LIBERALISM (insert conservative or progressive flavor here) and more Ockhamite degeneracy!

62467035
>>62462852
What they said is just boilerplate neoliberalism. It's the same ideology that got us into this mess. It's the rejection of virtue and wisdom, and ultimately God, in the search for freedom as sheer lack of constraint. It is, in essence, slavery to the appetites. It's progressive form is not really any different from its "conservative" form. It is, as John Milbank points out, really just a hersey.

You want a real solution, look at the Republic of Florence under Savonarola. That's what a real alternative looks like. The Flood is upon us. The Scourge of the Lord will fall heavy on our decadent and degenerate society. We must build the Ark of the Lord to ride out the storm.

62467152
>>62467010
Strawman cope.

62467662
>>62467152
>Socialism fails over and over and results in dystopias
>"This proves socialism is shit!"
>Neoliberalism gains global hegemony and is practiced over and over for half a century, resulting in a shitty dystopia.
>"We just need more neoliberalism. Real neoliberalism has never been tried!"

They are just the left and right hands of Satan. Both are based on hertical metaphysics and anthropology.

62467672
>>62467035
Savonarola was incredibly based.

62467797
>>62459782
Rhetoric like this makes it clear what a treacherous ponzi scheme the stock market really is. "It's at the top, so half the country needs to buy in now. It's called living in the real world, ya dolts."

>>62459926
>You can't "eat the rich" your way out of this
>t. lukewarm libertarian
Nice try.

>You need mass production
Not really. Mass markets should be completely eliminated.

>>62460013
Don't be a war profiteer next time.

>>62460557
>How retarded do you have to be to reach the conclusion that having more money to start and letting it sit somewhere and doing nothing yourself is actually the healthiest output for society?
It's called hubris, which is a fancy word for lying and coping. Stockfags don't know how not to do this.

62469330
>>62459782
The market will never bear enough interest to counter the credit card debt 95% of americans have.

62469351
>>62459782
That's not the Bill I know
>FUCK IT, WE'LL YOLO SOXL

62469369
>>62459782

item.title: Pandemic meta — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Pandemic meta

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-07-11 04:12 UTC

OP

Seeing as how the alt season failed, and chuds got left holding alt bags, it's not strange a man would adapt to the circumstances and find a new niche.. "news meta" . Basically what might occur in the news, whether hantavirus, a diarrhea outbreak or a parasite infestation in the US like Screwworm, solana and memecoins are still useful for quick gains that depend on narrative traction. Thoughts?

Early replies (first 3)

62468890
>>62468835
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YD3QKLOjKGM

62468908
>>62468835
Ok so what is your strategy?

62468927
>>62468835
memecoins are like 99.99% rugs and scams, so basically you have a 0.01% chance to make it with meme coins, nigga your better off just taking your money to a casino and bettering it all on a roulette number. trust me i found this out the hard way lmao

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62469011
>>62468927
You forget to factor in strategy my dude

62469024
>>62468890
More wise than the entire board.

62469063
There's a cattle crisis between USA and mexico, unfortunately there's money to be made

62469404
>>62468835
Jannies are banning threads about the robinhood chain memecoins. Not denying it is a shitcoin casino, but some are doing numbers rn. Made a 3x so far

item.title: I'm convinced most of the "it's over" crowd isn't representative of reality. — 27 replies — 13.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
I'm convinced most of the "it's over" crowd isn't representative of reality.

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-07-11 02:21 UTC

OP

I'm convinced most of the "it's over" crowd isn't representative of reality. The guy who got a 4% raise, paid off his car, bought his first house, or maxed his Roth IRA doesn't make a thread because there's nothing interesting to say. The guy who blames AI, recruiters, HR, offshoring, boomers, landlords, and capitalism for every setback posts 20 times a day.

At some point you have to ask yourself...maybe you're not oppressed by the economy. Maybe you just aren't that competitive.

Early replies (first 3)

62461507
>>62461450
Whenever everything is completely negative like this is just keep DCAing. Perception is always changing and I hate being sidelined. Better than USD anyway

62461519
>>62461450
I have a good salary, an apartment I own, no debt, regular raises matched to inflation at my stable government job, and if I want to I can stay there until retirement probably.

But I still like to bitch and moan when line go down. I took a 2% hit to my net worth recently and I'll make sure to tell you in every thread. The reality of it? It doesn't affect me at all. I still have my savings, I still have the rest of my portfolio, I'll make it back no problem. But it hurts my monkey brain. Monkeys HATE when you take shit away from them.

62461568
>>62461450
Gayest thread I've ever read

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62461586
>>62461450
You've got a site full of contrarians rejected by society, of course they're going to criticize a system that actively tries to make them fail.

If you don't feel like society hates you for one reason or another I'd have to wonder what you're even doing on 4chan. There are plenty of websites overtly dedicated to normies and the perks of being one of them.

62461616
>>62461568
Found the chronic doomposting faggot

62461650
>>62461450
>there's nothing interesting to say

...because not many people can relate.

62461664
>>62461586

I just like the humour here

62461679
>life objectively sucks and is continually getting worse for 99.999% of people
>noooooo, stop talking about it, goy!
Shalom!

62461774
Huh so the top 10% who can afford shit and the 35% who pretend to afford shit don't make threads or if they do, they post "it's actually not over" so the 55% who have been totally fucked should stop complaining. Definitely OP, we should stop noticing that wages have stagnated for 25 years and 99% of jobs will reject you.

62461777
>>62461586
>contrarians rejected by society
Engineers being paid 65k starting for 20 y ears without sizable increases in pay is not rejection, that's wage stagnation during massive inflation.

You aren't going to pilpul your way out of this, faggot.

62461786
>>62461777
globalization allowing people in india to do your job for 1/10th your salary is a very specific case where society has rejected and disadvantaged you.

of course you're angery and want to blame some vague ethnicity for your rejection. But that just causes society to reject you even further and gives them an excuse.
>I don't like indian engis but at least they're not angery and racist like murrican ones!

if it helps any all those AI's (Actual Indians) are going to get replaced by AI's

62461794
>>62461786
Blah blah blah

Society is worse and you want people to stop noticing because you're a faggot.

62461840
>>62461450
They're usually just foreigners

62462126
>he has nothing interesting to say
You wouldn't do anything about it on my behalf, zogged out as you are. Only I can save myself, and the morass of idiots online and in person serve only to keep the crabs in the bucket by design.

Information networks are flooded by bots and bad-faith actors who serve only to muddy the waters of truth. What would I gain by participating? More GME/shitcoin fomo for a cheap buck? I'm fine.

62462829
>>62461450
K-shaped economy means only the top 10% are doing well, the bottom 90% might not all be doomers but they're doomed nonetheless

62462982
>>62461777
Is not this, it's this! Chatgpt sounding faggot

62463198
>>62462982
He’s right, faggot

62463209
>>62461450
i got a raise to $150k this year. i still think it's over for the USA and most wagies.

62463349
>>62461450
>4% raise
That's called a pay cut.

62463370
>>62461450
>I'm convinced
Stopped reading there

62463379
What gay fucking aids thread is this. Yeah no shit dude and the people who defend the system the most are clearly those who stand to get guillotined by the masses if they don't get two hots a cot and a smartphone.

62463472
>>62461450
>The guy who got a 4% raise
Reminder that this guy is barely preserving the value of his income after considering inflation

62463485
>>62461519
>government job
how and what kind? I thought government jobs were reserved for women and browns

62463869
>>62461679
I’m doing pretty well. sounds like >>62461450 and >>62461519 are too. that’s over 10% of the thread, which is obviously indicative of society as a whole. cool it with your 0.001% doomerism

62466737
>>62461450
TRVTHNVKE

62466763
>>62461450
>The guy who got a 4% raise
doesn't exist. Timmy gets 2% and Chad got 12% and a fixed bonus or simply promoted

62469241
>>62461616
Yeah, lol

XRP and SWIFT going live.

XRP settling cross-border payments on a network that moves $150 trillion annually. The old rails are not being replaced — they are being upgraded with XRP running through them.

62468887

item.title: isnt this kinda sussy baka — 4 replies — 5.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
isnt this kinda sussy baka

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-07-11 03:39 UTC

OP

why is btc have value if xmr is needed to purchase things on dark web but btc is just a coin and is not needed. xmr is needed and therefore has more value, right? forgive me if im wrong but, is the reason BTC is still alive just because it's the one that got all the attention at the start?

also anons check with ur library to see if theres a morningstar dashboard, might be nice if there is.

btw has anyone here looked into cs2 markets or anything? i know it sounds crazy, but people think crypto is crazy too.

Early replies (first 3)

62468595
>>62468583
Xmr is for midwits to hold and high IQ individuals to use like the utility it is. You wouldn't understand my simpleton friend

62468770
>>62468583
My friend makes money on the side with CS2 market flips. Waits for update news, buys up shit that's cheap but won't be because of some bullshit changes, then just waits. Chinks pump up the prices like crazy and he benefits.

62468973
>>62468583
Because bitcoin's price is based on speculation
if it's price was based on use it wouldn't even be a 100 dollars

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62469362
>>62468583
Monero started replacing BTC for real world use in 2016. Coincidentally, now that XMR totally dominates that market, it's about the same price as BTC was in 2016. (Which is only relevant because they have similar supplies so market caps are comparable)

In 2017 BTC was mainly the gateway to altcoins (ponzi scams). Ethereum and Tether have taken over that role for the most part now.

Bitcoin price today is a mixture of whales colluding with each other (including high finance and government) while fostering a sizeable cult of paypigs who put large portions of their paychecks into BTC every week in the belief that it will be worth something someday.

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-07-11 01:21 UTC

OP

Robinhood chain went live recently. Tokens are already sending to 1m-100m+ range based on the concept of how new the ecosystem is despite it being a recycled EVM wrapper.

Forget about new ideas or doing something important. Let's piss off a community that's already established on solana by making a token with the same name go parabolic on the Robinhood chain.

Early replies (first 3)

62466199
>>62466198

62466215

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2 — 81 replies — 82.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2

Open thread • Replies: 81 • Updated: 2026-07-11 03:21 UTC

OP

Brothers,

We spent 20 days and 203 posts together in the last thread and we have yet to formulate a method that will enable us to sit at the table in the six figure club with the rest of kinsmen. We knew this wouldn’t be easy so let's not get dispassionate.

For my next proposal I will say we focus on developing a skill that will increase our value. If we all level up on our own it will only take longer. If we all level up by 2-3 points a month then cumulatively we will all have gotten stronger as a mass society. Perhaps one amongst us can elevate themselves and come back to teach the others!

How long shall we be waiting then? How long will our own potential be mocked by others? To what limit will we continue to persist in the underclass?

Come, those who have not $10,000 to their name

Let us commence the general!

>Good /biz/ manga
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Good /biz/ movies
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Born_Rich_(2003_film)

>Good /biz/ books
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Gambler_(novel)

>Good /biz/ games
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Good /biz/ waifus
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elizabeth_Holmes

>Agenda 2030 is on the way, have you studied what your enemy has planned out?
https://www.math.uwaterloo.ca/~ervrscay/TheGreatReset.pdf

Praeviously
>>62373752

Early replies (first 3)

62453729
All you faggots need to do is buy chainlink. Fuck your side hustles. You can get a suicide stack for 8k right now.

62453795
Last thread we spoke a lot on learning new skills but very few fruitful things arose. I realized the issue is that a lot of the proposed skills were just boring or too niche.

62453799
>>62453795
wait, are you asking poor people to discuss amongst themselves how to get rich?
kek

you don't learn how to get rich from poor people

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62462799
>>62457657
Must you broadcast your ethnic insecurity all over 4channel.org(slash) biz?

62462804
>>62454046
>mom look I posted it again!

62462813
I finally paid off my high Apr CC debt so now I can stack paychecks for a while. I’ve been good about cutting down spending in general. I wonder if I should cut out meat and just do rice and beans? Can you get similar nutrition from beans? Things are certainly not looking great because I care about my job less by the day but at least I paid off debt.

I don’t wanna /blog too hard but fuck life is sucking lately, I just hope it’s better in a year. I been working nonstop for two years haven’t had any pto either

62462824
>>62453622
>yet to formulate a method that will enable us to sit at the table in the six figure club with the rest of kinsmen
go work literally any job for like 6 months

62462992
>UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2

OH NO NO NO

AHAHAHAHAHAHA

LMAOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

62464145
>>62462813
Rent truly is the worst bloodsucker. What are you saving for? Just to have reserve funds? You were spending all your excess money on debt and not saving/investing? Genuinely asking

62464163
>>62462813
Also I would be careful with poor nutrition depending on your age. If under 25 then take the risk with cheap foods.

62465232
Bump, almost done waging

62465510
alright today, lets practice looking at good designs of top runners and seeing if we can emulate the artists ability. Let's also look at great /biz/ memes and ask ourselves, ""what made this meme so popular" like it was an actual gallery

62465526
Who is this? What makes him so special? Why is he one of the most identifiable mascots on this site?

62465529
Forgot the image, what about this characters DESIGN made him so well loved

62465547
this is what knowyourmeme has to say on Mumu

side note: alt girls get paid to make videos like this? what a job!

62465548
what will happen when we reach #10k thread?

62465553
the absolute state of this board

62465556
>>62453622
Quads and I’ll chop my cock off

62465561
>>62465556
dude...

62465736
How did this character become the most recognizable image on /biz/?

62465765
and here is information on bobo

62465874
I realize I have lost some anons attentions, let us have a group activity. Let's all draw a variation of Bobo or Mumu. I will go ahead and start!

62465886
>>62465874
make sure you use a Bobo or Mumu as reference!

62466308
>>62465547
>alt girls get paid to make videos like this?
she's been making videos about memes for around 2 years, I guess she just got a deal with KYM now. I want to have sex with her btw

62466618
Fell asleep, will continue this tomorrow

62467689
>grandpa was a millionaire when he died in the 90s
>fucked up his will, his children squandered what was left of it
>everything, to the last cent, gone in two generations
>I get the shitpie of go fuck yourself

I hope the pod is cozy

62467752
>>62467689
This is why you have to skip generations

62468039
>>62453622
>poor OP doesn't understand one of /biz/'s basic memes, mumu

62469068
finally, off work

62469071
>>62453622
lmao holy shit biz is fucked

62469114
>>62469071
here is my picture of mumu!
what do you guys think?

How much money could this net me? Could it

get me a job?
be a nft worth thousands?
make it into a popular meme?

what could I have done better, where was I lacking? feel free to post your own Bobo or Mumu, and we can all see where we need to improve. even we are good at drawing, we don't know where to market it. We might be good at marketing, but poor at drawing. Perhaps there's something my Mumu lacks that is holding it back from being something that others would want to save(remember this, if they save your art, they must like it somewhat)

62469339
here is a picture of my Bobo! Didn't take too long, anyone here could pull it off, feel free to show me yours, come on guys!

That being said there is something missing from my work

item.title: VOO and Chill — 220 replies — 45.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 220 • Updated: 2026-07-10 23:27 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62455990
>>62455475
Look into VPMAX

62456048
>>62455990
Neat

62459187
>>62403053
The median 64 yo is planning to work until they're dead.
Boomers are never going to retire because they can't afford to. They are going to keep working like the good little goyim they are and fall down dead on the job because that's the only way they can fund the monthly payments on all the toys they went into debt to purchase.
I spit on their graves. Fuck boomer scum.

62459238
>>62459187
Lmao, another white incel who knows everything,,,,,what are the odds? 100% at least,,,,,do they use it to get rich? Lmao no

62460695
Yawwwwwn another week another VOO purchase

62460733
>>62460695
i made a whole $5 buying a share of VOO during that dip :) too bad I lost more from all my previous VOO shares

62460835
>>62460733
Sorry to hear that champ maybe DRAM is more your speed

62460844
>>62400995
Lmfao. You do realize that the highest weighted stocks that lead the spy are mostly in QQQ right? Lmfao

62460852
>>62459187
They can afford it

62461238
>>62401051
>I think a decade long CAGR comparison was a 5% difference but I’m not sure if CAGR is the best stat to go by?
It changes significantly depending on when you enter the market. Since 2000 there's only a 0.6% difference in favor of QQQ over SPY, the latter outperformed the former for most of that timeframe. If you bought sometime post-2009, however, you would have been better off holding QQQ by far. The moral of the story is keep an eye on things and only buy more volatile assets if it's a growth period. QQQ will continue to be great if you bought a decade ago until the music stops.

62461412
>>62400515
Fpbp. Bogleheads are the biggest retards on the planet.

62461672
>>62400620
>What makes one rich
Health, family, friends.

62462476
>>62461238
For now I’m still just doing VOO cause it just feels better even if I’m missing out on qqq gains

62464487
It’s payday tomorrow, VOO brahs. There’s nothing risky that looks too appealing so I go all voo maybe some palantir as well. My only regret is taking the work health benefits, my check will be smaller. Two weeks is an eternity to wait for your money.

62464830
Is there any point of voo and chill if I already have a 401k and pension. Know can compound it more but can be more risky right

62466388
>>62400493
buy lottery tickets

62467200
>>62466388
But VOO, QQQM if you want more return and volatility

62467206
>>62460835
no no i don't buy dogshit. thanks

62467215
>>62464830
retire earlier
imagine if you have enough VOO in your brokerage that you can afford to live off its dividends only. you'll pay virtually no tax and enjoy 10% growth per year indefinitely.

62467367
>>62467206
I was being sarcastic I’m a Boglehead

62468767
>>62461672
Am i rich if i have health, family, and friends but i'm stuck wageslaving 50 hours a week and perpetually stressed and taking orders like a bitch and doing endless donkey work just to watch my bosses and clients get more money and freedom than me?

62468775
>>62400493
Buying voo only makes sense if you have size.

62468793
>>62468775
Is 7”x5” enough?

62468838
Reckless strategy. Practically gambling. Do you even ACWI IMI?

62468882
>>62468767
The great majority of people need to work to live. And there are many miserable "rich" people and many happy "poor" people. You will come to learn.

62468885
How much are you guys DCA'ing into the shit and piss 500? I'm doing about $2,500 every monthy and I'm at a little over $780k.

62468889
>>62468885
Idk 12% of my paycheck for however long it's been I don't look at it

62468891
>>62468885
Also, I have noticed that the people complaining about the returns from the S&P500 are people who don't have a job and only have a lump sum to invest.

62468920
i got 100k saved up from wageslaving, how do invest it to chill and earn with etf's?

also should i buy these etf directly from vanguard?

62468957
>>62400636
This was me at 35 just doing the bare minimum to match at my company. It wasn’t 150 but 280 but you get the idea.

I’m now 39 and maxing out my HSA/Roth IRA/Roth 403b/bonds. It’s close to 60k a year I’m putting way and I still have 20 years to catch up.

It can be done but so many of my peers are barely saving and living above there means it’s crazy.

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 248 replies — 40.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 248 • Updated: 2026-07-10 22:14 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62464891
>>62464772
The haters are already watching Kek. They can’t stop talking about his wife in both threads and they’re literally reading court filings for his divorce.

62464895
>>62464891
They are convinced that Sonichu is both PP and ABC in disguise.

62464897
>>62464895
PP HAS to be every towel knower.

62464909
>>62464895
>everyone is PP
I am not PP, debunked. Shills can not dispute the holiday inn dilemma

62464919
>>62464772
$GABBBYQ has already won, this is why she hasn’t been since the 18th

62464943
I’m starting to think this is a larp

62464998
>>62464943
>The CUSIP Conundrum, the Director Dilemma, and the Affable Affiliates are all an elaborate ruse.

62465011
>>62464998
>The Gabbbyq Gape at the Holiday Inn
Interesting

62465133
>>62464943
What does live action role playing have to do with the bankruptcy of bed bath and beyond?

62465151
>>62464047
that's the thing about starting a religion. You have to figure out how to monetize it

so he creates a proxy for BBBY and then pretends it's being crucified by the romans just like your messiah was. Then he can trick some of you fools into buying it before rugging you like the dumbass sheep you are.

>>62464998
those things are obvious schizophrenic apophenia. What's not clear is if your schizophrenia is real or affected.

62465330
The shills were instructed to spam shit about the personal life of theppshow host because pulte(director of national intelligence) frequenting it and Larry cheng going on after shares were cancelled, were huge signals that told retail bbbyq is coming back.

Even more important is that the cusip-6 of bbbyq and the bbby warrants match. Shillies stopped having their mods frantically delete posts last fall/winter and now they just spam and flood to try and avoid the curiosity altogether.

62465397
>>62465330
Ok debunk the divorce filings in Utah and why Mrs PP has not been seen since the 18th and Ryan Cohen tweeted a T Rex image in the Bahamas

You can’t shilly willy

62465412
all they have left is forced satire and inauthentic contrived meme energy that never sticks which is why the AMC spam (attempt to discredit the CUSIP-6) stopped because this stuff never works and people see right through it.

62465418
>>62465412
silo the topic as much as you want, you can’t refute the holiday inn

62465422
>>62465418
I guess I'll take the bait.

You think focusing on the shows host is more important that the content discussed on the show? Or prominent figures who went onto the show who are very close to RC? Of course you don't because you're paid to be here.

62465431
>>62465422
I fully agree with you, Ryan has been inside the PP suite at the holiday inn as well, we all have. We are all waiting 9 months now for the divorce to conclude and we can all celebrate, at the holiday inn.

62465456
You'd think the shill sissies would be uploading these divorce documents or whatever the fuck they're talking about since they already uploaded PP's resume and everything else. Makes you wonder if they're just...making things up with zero basis whatsoever. Hard to blame them cause these boomers and jeets really can't meme. Between dougshit, CUSIP Conundrum, etc. they really have no way of controlling sentiment so they just make the lamest copies trying to capture any bit of meme energy.

62465498
>>62465456
Do your own research, the cuck chair rarely comes pre heated, but when it does, PP is there.

Funny how you haven’t disproven the (1) thing yet you refuse discuss, interesting

62465544
>>62465456

62465594
>>62465544
>no mention of his devoted wife
Divorce is ongoing, nothing happens until it concludes.

GME bulls have won

62465636
>>62465151
>your messiah
Kek

62466545
CUSIP-6

62466579
>>62466545
>ignores the real conundrum
GME Bulls are in control

62466875
>>62466579

62466937
>>62466875
>everything depends on GME bulls
>the GABBBYQ KEKS need fantasty RP for their theory

62467452
Hello friends. Are we tuning in to Jake's space call today? Should be lots of fun!

62467493
>>62467452
Thank you for the reminder, King! I almost forgot!

62467912
Jake2SNEED is the goat.

62468829
>>62467912
He is very based. No other stock community has anyone close to Jake. He is the bbbyq champion.

62468834
Nothing happens until the divorce is finalised and GABBBYQ has controlling interests on the board

GME bulls are in charge now
